summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements explicit provider isolation by clearing all provider-specific settings before building provider invocation, with boundary enforcement via snapshot-based mutation detection.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider settings and explicitly clears Claude-specific settings for Claude provider, preventing cross-contamination from other providers
evidence.why: Provider isolation is critical to prevent Claude tool restriction flags from leaking into Codex or custom provider invocations.

evidence.summary: Claude provider branch explicitly resets settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools to empty strings and sets agent_tool_profile to normalized value
evidence.why: This ensures Claude evidence agents start with clean tool configuration instead of inheriting restrictions from global config.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime builds boundary snapshot before spawning any agents
evidence.why: Pre-execution snapshot is required to detect project mutations during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Post-agent snapshot diff generates boundary change list and violates readiness if any changes detected outside allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement blocks PRD readiness if agents mutate project files despite explicit instructions not to.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates explicit instructions: Boundary: do not modify target project files and allowed artifact root path
evidence.why: Agent prompts must explicitly forbid project mutations to pass boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation accepts normalized provider from executionConfig and builds provider-specific invocation without cross-provider logic
evidence.why: Provider adapter is stateless and relies on pre-normalized config, preventing cross-provider assumption drift.

evidence.summary: Claude invocation builds args from scratch using model, permission_mode, settings, tools, allowedTools, and other flags but all are passed empty or default by evidence-dispatch
evidence.why: Claude agent invocation preserves full tool capability by not passing restrictive tool flags.

evidence.summary: codex dispatch test asserts that provider is codex, codex_sandbox is undefined, and claude model pattern is not present in config
evidence.why: Test verifies Codex provider does not inherit Claude defaults or model assumptions.

evidence.summary: claude dispatch test asserts all tool restriction flags are empty and no_session_persistence is true
evidence.why: Test verifies Claude provider starts with clean tool configuration preserving full capability.

evidence.summary: boundary mutation test writes unexpected-project-write.txt and asserts status is blocked, project_mutation is violated, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker exists
evidence.why: Test validates boundary enforcement catches violations even when agent results pass.

evidence.summary: non-git boundary fallback test detects modified src/existing.txt and ignores agent-scratch.txt in allowed artifact root
evidence.why: Test validates boundary works without git and excludes allowed write roots from violation detection.

assumptions: Provider-adapter.ts buildProviderInvocation is stateless and does not cache or share assumptions between provider types.
Agent prompt instructions are respected by Claude/Codex/custom providers; no provider tool can bypass boundary enforcement.
Git ls-files with exclude-standard covers all relevant project files and gitignore patterns align with exclusion list.
risks: If executionConfig fails to normalize provider settings due to config loading errors, provider-specific defaults may leak.
If agent prompts ignore boundary instructions and mutate project files, PRD readiness will block but git working tree will be dirty.
If git boundary snapshot misses untracked files due to gitignore patterns, mutations may go undetected.

result.notes: Evidence confirms explicit provider isolation via executionConfig normalization and boundary enforcement via snapshot-based mutation detection. All three scope_in files implement and test the provider configuration preservation and cross-provider leakage prevention. No missing evidence identified.

agent_results.claim: VERIFIED: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements explicit provider isolation and boundary enforcement as claimed by the explorer, with all 10 evidence items independently confirmed through source code analysis.

evidence.summary: Claude provider branch in executionConfig explicitly resets all tool restriction flags (settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) to empty strings and sets claude_no_session_persistence to true, confirming provider isolation.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms explorer claim at lines 450-463: Claude evidence agents start with clean tool configuration.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates SHA-256 digest map of all project files excluding allowed_write_roots, implementing the pre-execution snapshot mechanism.
evidence.why: Confirms the snapshot infrastructure exists for boundary enforcement, supporting explorer claim about line 515-517.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files, providing the change detection logic.
evidence.why: Verifies the diff mechanism that detects mutations, supporting boundary enforcement claim at lines 566-572.

evidence.summary: Pre-execution boundary snapshot created before spawning any agents: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]).
evidence.why: Confirms exact location of pre-execution snapshot, matching explorer claim at lines 515-517.

evidence.summary: Post-agent snapshot diff detects violations: boundaryAfter snapshot compared to boundaryBefore, changes trigger BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms exact lines 566-572 as claimed by explorer for boundary violation detection.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates explicit boundary instructions: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Allowed artifact/scratch root: ${plan.output_dir}'.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim that agent prompts explicitly forbid project mutations with allowed artifact root path.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation is a pure function that accepts normalized provider from config.ai and builds provider-specific invocation without cross-provider logic or state.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim that provider adapter is stateless and prevents cross-provider assumption drift.

evidence.summary: Claude invocation args built from scratch including -p, --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools flags, all receiving empty or default values when not provided.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim that Claude agent invocation preserves full tool capability by not passing restrictive flags.

evidence.summary: Codex dispatch test verifies provider is codex, codex_sandbox is undefined, and claude model pattern is absent, testing provider isolation.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim that test validates Codex provider does not inherit Claude defaults.

evidence.summary: Claude dispatch test asserts all tool restriction flags are empty strings and no_session_persistence is true, verifying clean tool configuration.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim that test confirms Claude provider starts with clean tool configuration.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutation test writes unexpected-project-write.txt and asserts status is blocked, project_mutation is violated, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker exists.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim that test validates boundary enforcement catches violations even when agent results pass.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test detects modified src/existing.txt and ignores agent-scratch.txt in allowed artifact root, validating git-less boundary enforcement.
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim that test validates boundary works without git and excludes allowed write roots.

evidence.summary: walkFiles function excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories, confirming exclusion list used in snapshot building.
evidence.why: Verifies one of explorer assumptions about excluded directories in boundary snapshot.

assumptions: Git ls-files with exclude-standard covers all relevant project files and gitignore patterns align with security exclusion list.
Agent prompt instructions are respected by Claude/Codex/custom providers; no provider tool can bypass boundary enforcement.
Provider-adapter.ts buildProviderInvocation is stateless and does not cache or share assumptions between provider types.
risks: If executionConfig fails to normalize provider settings due to config loading errors, provider-specific defaults may leak.
If agent prompts ignore boundary instructions and mutate project files, PRD readiness will block but git working tree will be dirty.
If git boundary snapshot misses untracked files due to gitignore patterns, mutations may go undetected.

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all 10 evidence items from the explorer. Provider isolation via executionConfig clearing Claude-specific settings (lines 450-463) is verified. Boundary enforcement via snapshot-based mutation detection (lines 516, 566-572) is confirmed. All test assertions match source implementation. No conflicts detected between explorer claims and independent verification. The demand evidence dispatch system implements provider isolation and boundary enforcement as claimed.

agent_results.claim: VERIFIED: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient, consistent, and properly sourced to support PRD readiness claims. All 11 evidence items have project-scoped sources with exact paths and line ranges. Assumptions and risks are properly separated from facts. No missing data remains.

evidence.summary: Claude provider branch resets tool restriction flags (settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) to empty strings, confirming provider isolation implementation
evidence.why: Verifies that the primary provider isolation mechanism exists in code with exact line reference.

evidence.summary: Post-agent snapshot diff generates boundary change list and violates readiness if changes detected outside allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary enforcement logic exists and triggers PRD readiness blockers on violations.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates explicit boundary instructions: do not modify target project files, allowed artifact root path
evidence.why: Verifies that agent prompts include explicit boundary enforcement instructions.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutation tests verify that violations are detected both with and without git, and that allowed write roots are excluded from violation detection
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary enforcement has test coverage for violation scenarios.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation is a pure function accepting normalized provider, building provider-specific invocation without cross-provider logic
evidence.why: Verifies that provider adapter is stateless, preventing cross-provider assumption drift.

assumptions: Provider-adapter.ts buildProviderInvocation is stateless and does not cache or share assumptions between provider types.
Agent prompt instructions are respected by Claude/Codex/custom providers; no provider tool can bypass boundary enforcement.
Git ls-files with exclude-standard covers all relevant project files and gitignore patterns align with security exclusion list.
risks: If executionConfig fails to normalize provider settings due to config loading errors, provider-specific defaults may leak.
If agent prompts ignore boundary instructions and mutate project files, PRD readiness will block but git working tree will be dirty.
If git boundary snapshot misses untracked files due to gitignore patterns, mutations may go undetected.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is consistent, with all claims backed by project-scoped sources (code and tests) at exact paths and line ranges. Cross-checker independently verified all 11 evidence items with zero conflicts. Assumptions are properly separated from facts and not presented as verified claims. Risks are clearly articulated and reflect actual implementation concerns. All three required scope_in files (src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts, src/runtime/execution/provider-adapter.ts, __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts) have evidence coverage. No missing data remains; evidence is sufficient to unblock PRD readiness.
